overview.short: Alappancode Sree Easwarakala Bhoothathan Temple is a very ancient and known centre of pilgrimage for the devotees of Alappancode Ammavan (Eswarakala Bhoothathan).
overview.description: Alappancode Sree Easwarakala Bhoothathan Temple is a very ancient and known centre of pilgrimage for the devotees of Alappancode Ammavan (Eswarakala Bhoothathan).

topPlaces.name: Kilkulam
topPlaces.description: Keezhkulam is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the India state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Pacode
topPlaces.description: Pacode is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Kuzhithurai
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kuzhithura is a diocese located in Tamil Nadu. It was erected on 22 December 2014 by Pope Francis with territory taken from the Diocese of Kottar.

topPlaces.name: Manjalumoodu
topPlaces.description: Manjalumoodu is a village in the Kanniyakumari district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Chitharal Jain Monuments and Bhagavati Temple
topPlaces.description: The Chitharal Jain Temple and Bhagavati Temple are located near Chitharal village in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. They consist of stone beds with inscriptions, and two monuments – one rock cut Jain temple with outer wall reliefs and one Hindu goddess temple next to it that is a combination of rock-cut and stone that was added during the reign of Vikramaditya Varaguna Pandya.

image.alt: Chitharal Jain Monuments and Bhagavati Temple

topPlaces.name: Moovattumugam
topPlaces.description: Moovattumugam is a hamlet in India situated 2 km northwest of Thiruvattar, where the rivers Kothai and Pahrali unify to flow southwest towards the Arabian Sea. An ancient Sastha Temple is situated the bank of Moovattumugom.

topPlaces.name: Vilavancode
topPlaces.description: Vilavancode, also spelt as Viḷavaṅgōḍu, is a town panchayat in Kanyakumari district in Tamil Nadu state, India. It is part of territory among several taluks that were with the Thiruvananthapuram district that with the passage of the States Reorganisation Act of 1956 transferred from Thiruvananthapuram district, Travancore-Cochin State to the newly created Kanyakumari district of Madras State (the latter later renamed as Tamil Nadu State).

topPlaces.name: Padanthalumoodu
topPlaces.description: Padanthalumoodu is located in Kanyakumari District of Tamil Nadu. The area of Padanthalumoodu lies in the taluk of Vilavancode and has a Sub Post Office.

items.note: Keezhkulam is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the India state of Tamil Nadu.
Pacode is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kuzhithura is a diocese located in Tamil Nadu. It was erected on 22 December 2014 by Pope Francis with territory taken from the Diocese of Kottar.

items.activity: Chitharal Jain Monuments and Bhagavati Temple
items.note: The Chitharal Jain Temple and Bhagavati Temple are located near Chitharal village in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. They consist of stone beds with inscriptions, and two monuments – one rock cut Jain temple with outer wall reliefs and one Hindu goddess temple next to it that is a combination of rock-cut and stone that was added during the reign of Vikramaditya Varaguna Pandya.

Moovattumugam is a hamlet in India situated 2 km northwest of Thiruvattar, where the rivers Kothai and Pahrali unify to flow southwest towards the Arabian Sea. An ancient Sastha Temple is situated the bank of Moovattumugom.

Vilavancode, also spelt as Viḷavaṅgōḍu, is a town panchayat in Kanyakumari district in Tamil Nadu state, India. It is part of territory among several taluks that were with the Thiruvananthapuram district that with the passage of the States Reorganisation Act of 1956 transferred from Thiruvananthapuram district, Travancore-Cochin State to the newly created Kanyakumari district of Madras State (the latter later renamed as Tamil Nadu State).
Padanthalumoodu is located in Kanyakumari District of Tamil Nadu. The area of Padanthalumoodu lies in the taluk of Vilavancode and has a Sub Post Office.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (35 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (33 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alappancode Easwara Kala Bhoothathan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kilkulam, Pacode, Diocese of Kuzhithurai, Manjalumoodu, Chitharal Jain Monuments and Bhagavati Temple.
